foundations.title: Design Principles
foundations.content: Our design system is guided by four principles that shape every decision we make.

sections.title: Clarity
sections.body: Every element should communicate its purpose immediately. If a user has to think about what something does, we have failed. Labels are explicit. Icons are accompanied by text. States are visually unambiguous.

sections.title: Consistency
sections.body: The same pattern should work the same way everywhere. A button in a modal behaves identically to a button on a page. Consistency reduces the learning curve and builds user confidence over time.

sections.title: Accessibility
sections.body: Every component must be usable by everyone, regardless of ability or context. We design for keyboard users, screen reader users, and users in challenging environments. Accessibility is not an afterthought — it is built in from the start.

sections.title: Efficiency
sections.body: Reduce cognitive load and unnecessary steps. Every additional element on a screen competes for attention. We include only what serves the user's task and remove everything else.

foundations.title: Grid & Layout
foundations.content: Our layout system uses a consistent grid to ensure content is readable, aligned, and adaptable across screen sizes.

sections.title: Column Grid
sections.body: We use a 12-column grid with 24px gutters on desktop. Content is centred within a 1200px max-width container. This gives flexibility for a wide range of layout patterns without requiring custom spacing.

sections.title: Breakpoints
sections.body: Mobile: 0–599px. Tablet: 600–1023px. Desktop: 1024px and above. All components are designed mobile-first and scale up progressively. Never build a component that works only at one breakpoint.

sections.title: Spacing Scale
sections.body: All spacing uses the defined spacing tokens (xs through 2xl). Do not use arbitrary pixel values. This ensures visual rhythm is maintained across the product and makes global adjustments possible.

foundations.title: Accessibility
foundations.content: All components in this design system meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards as a minimum requirement.

sections.title: Colour Contrast
sections.body: Minimum contrast ratio is 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text and UI components. All colour combinations used in the system have been tested and pass at AA level. Never introduce new colour pairings without testing contrast first.

sections.title: Keyboard Navigation
sections.body: All interactive elements are reachable and operable via keyboard. Tab order follows visual reading order. Focus states are always visible — we never suppress the default focus outline without providing a custom alternative.

sections.title: Screen Readers
sections.body: All non-text content has a text alternative. Icon-only buttons carry an aria-label. Form fields are associated with their labels. Dynamic content changes are announced via ARIA live regions where appropriate.
